project.name: PropManager Design & Best Practices Consolidation
project.description: Systematic elimination of technical debt, code duplication, and architectural inconsistencies in the PropManager property management SaaS. This PRD addresses 23 items across notification consolidation, component consolidation, backend architecture, code quality, and performance optimization.

qualityStandards.description: Technical debt degrades velocity exponentially. This PRD focuses on high-impact consolidation work that reduces maintenance burden, improves developer experience, and establishes clear patterns for future development.
principles: Single source of truth for all shared functionality
Type safety catches bugs at compile time, not runtime
Authorization belongs in policies, not controllers
Configuration over hardcoding for business values
Components should be reusable and well-typed

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

commands.typecheck: npm run build -- --mode development 2>&1 | grep -i error

notification_consolidation.description: Consolidate notification system architecture to eliminate confusion between NotificationPreference, Setting, and 4 different UI locations

component_consolidation.description: Eliminate duplicate Vue components and establish single-source-of-truth shared components

backend_architecture.description: Extract shared controller logic, implement missing policies, and consolidate validation rules

code_quality.description: Remove console statements, add typed exceptions, improve TypeScript usage

performance_optimization.description: Fix N+1 queries, add caching for expensive operations, optimize bulk operations

items.title: Create Unified Notification Configuration Architecture
items.description: The notification system has conceptual confusion: NotificationPreference stores per-user preferences, Setting stores provider credentials, but landlord's NotificationPreference is treated as 'defaults for new tenants'. This creates maintenance nightmares and user confusion. Create a clear architectural separation.
steps: Audit current notification tables: notification_preferences, settings (notifications category)
Design new schema: notification_provider_config (landlord provider settings), notification_defaults (landlord default preferences for tenants), notification_preferences (individual user preferences)
Create migration to restructure tables while preserving existing data
Update NotificationService to use new architecture
Update NotificationPreference model to only handle user preferences
Create NotificationProviderConfig model for provider credentials
Create NotificationDefaults model for landlord-level defaults
Update all controllers referencing notification settings
definition_of_ready: Current notification data flow fully documented
New schema design reviewed and approved
Migration strategy for existing data defined
acceptance_criteria: Clear separation: provider config vs defaults vs user preferences
Landlord can configure providers in ONE location only
Landlord can set tenant defaults in ONE location only
Tenants can override preferences in their profile
definition_of_done: Migration runs successfully with data preservation
No references to old dual-purpose patterns remain
items.verification: Send notification to tenant; verify routing uses correct preference hierarchy (user > defaults > system)
items.phase_progress: Phase 6 of 6 complete. All phases implemented including CodeRabbit bug fixes.

phases_completed.phase_1: Create notification config architecture tables and models
phases_completed.phase_2: Repository pattern for notification config with dual-write
phases_completed.phase_4: Controller migration and feature flag flip
phases_completed.phase_5: Fix OR/AND logic bug in isEmailConfiguredLegacy (CodeRabbit issue)
phases_completed.phase_6: Fix partial update inconsistency in credential setters (CodeRabbit issue)

items.title: Consolidate Notification Settings UI to Single Location
items.description: Notification settings are scattered across 4 UI locations: Settings/NotificationsTab (landlord defaults), Notifications/SettingsTab (provider config), Profile/NotificationsTab (user preferences), Admin/Settings (system). Consolidate to 2 clear locations: Notifications Center (providers + defaults) and Profile (user preferences).
steps: Audit all 4 notification UI locations and document what each controls
Design consolidated UI: Notifications/Index.vue becomes the single landlord destination
Remove Settings/partials/NotificationsTab.vue - redirect to Notifications Center
Keep Profile/Partials/NotificationsTab.vue for user-specific preferences only
Update navigation to reflect new structure
Add clear section headers distinguishing Provider Config vs Tenant Defaults
Implement breadcrumbs for clear navigation context
definition_of_ready: DBP-001 (architecture consolidation) completed or in parallel
UI/UX mockups approved for consolidated views
User journey documented for landlord vs tenant
acceptance_criteria: Landlord has exactly ONE location for notification configuration
Tenant has exactly ONE location for notification preferences
Clear visual distinction between provider settings and preference defaults
Navigation is intuitive with no dead ends
definition_of_done: Old notification settings tabs removed or redirected
E2E test for landlord notification configuration flow
E2E test for tenant preference customization flow
items.verification: As landlord, navigate to notification settings; verify single destination with all options
items.completion_notes: Consolidated from 4 UI locations to 2: (1) Admin/Settings.vue now only shows Payment Gateway with info banner directing to Notifications Center for Email/SMS; (2) Notifications/SettingsTab.vue renamed 'Defaults & Archive' tab to 'Archive' and removed duplicate default preferences (now only in Settings > Notifications); (3) Settings/NotificationsTab.vue unchanged as canonical location for tenant defaults; (4) Profile/NotificationsTab.vue unchanged for tenant push subscriptions. Backend: Removed 4 methods from AdminController (updateEmailSettings, testEmailConnection, updateSmsSettings, testSmsConnection) and corresponding routes.

items.title: Centralize Quiet Hours Logic
items.description: Quiet hours implementation is scattered across NotificationPreference model, NotificationService, and frontend components. Consolidate to a single QuietHoursService with clear API.
steps: Create QuietHoursService with methods: isQuietHours(), getNextDeliveryTime(), shouldDefer()
Move isInQuietHours() and getQuietHoursEnd() from NotificationPreference to service
Update NotificationService to use QuietHoursService
Update ProcessScheduledNotifications command to use service
Create QuietHoursConfig value object for type-safe configuration
Add unit tests for all timezone edge cases
definition_of_ready: All current quiet hours logic locations identified
acceptance_criteria: Single service handles all quiet hours logic
definition_of_done: QuietHoursService created with full test coverage
Old model methods marked deprecated with migration path
All callers updated to use new service
items.verification: Unit test: 23:00 with 22:00-08:00 quiet hours returns true; 12:00 returns false

items.title: Consolidate MetricCard Components
items.description: Two MetricCard components exist: Components/MetricCard.vue (simpler, no TypeScript) and Components/Finances/MetricCard.vue (more features, TypeScript, uses formatters). Consolidate to single well-typed component.
steps: Audit all usages of both MetricCard components across the codebase
Design consolidated API merging best features from both
Update Components/MetricCard.vue to match Finances/MetricCard.vue feature set
Add TypeScript types to consolidated component
Migrate all usages to use the consolidated component
Delete Components/Finances/MetricCard.vue
Update component index exports
definition_of_ready: All MetricCard usages identified and documented
acceptance_criteria: Single MetricCard component handles all use cases
Uses useFormatters composable for all formatting
Supports all existing features: trends, icons, colors, links, loading states
definition_of_done: All pages using the consolidated component
items.verification: Build succeeds; visual regression test shows no UI changes
items.completion_notes: Consolidated MetricCard: (1) Replaced Components/MetricCard.vue with TypeScript version featuring useFormatters, 8-color palette, format prop (currency/number/percent/text), loading state; (2) Migrated 5 dashboard files (Dashboard, Admin/Dashboard, Buildings/Dashboard, Caretaker/Dashboard, Tenant/Dashboard) from iconBgColor/iconColor to unified color prop; (3) Updated Finances/index.ts to re-export from @/Components/MetricCard.vue; (4) Deleted Components/Finances/MetricCard.vue; (5) npm build and pint pass.

items.title: Consolidate Pagination Components
items.description: Two Pagination components exist: Components/Pagination.vue (basic, uses Link) and Components/Finances/Pagination.vue (TypeScript, uses router.visit, conditional rendering). Consolidate to single flexible component.
steps: Audit all pagination usages across the codebase
Design consolidated API supporting both navigation methods
Implement single Pagination.vue with TypeScript
Add prop for navigation method (link vs router)
Migrate all usages to consolidated component
Delete Components/Finances/Pagination.vue
Update component index exports
definition_of_ready: Edge cases documented (empty, single page, many pages)
acceptance_criteria: Supports both Link-based and router.visit navigation
items.verification: Navigate pagination on multiple pages; verify consistent behavior

items.title: Create Unified Badge Component System
items.description: Multiple badge components exist with inconsistent patterns: KycBadge, TicketStatusBadge, InvoiceStatusBadge, PaymentMethodBadge, TicketPriorityBadge. Create a unified Badge component with status-specific presets.
steps: Audit all badge components and extract common patterns
Design unified Badge.vue with preset system
Create status color mappings in useStatusColors composable
Implement Badge.vue with TypeScript and preset support
Create specific wrappers: StatusBadge.vue (for statuses), MethodBadge.vue (for payment methods)
Migrate existing badges to use new system
Delete old badge components
definition_of_ready: Color palette for all statuses defined
acceptance_criteria: Single Badge component with color/size/variant props
Preset system for common statuses (invoice, payment, ticket, kyc)
definition_of_done: All old badges migrated or deleted
items.verification: Visual regression test; all badge usages render correctly

items.title: Promote Finances/EmptyState to Shared Component
items.description: Finances/EmptyState.vue is well-designed but only available in Finances module. Many pages have ad-hoc empty states. Promote to shared component and standardize all empty states.
steps: Move EmptyState.vue from Components/Finances/ to Components/
Update imports in all Finances pages
Identify all inline empty states in codebase
Migrate inline empty states to use shared component
Add additional size variants if needed
Update component documentation
acceptance_criteria: Supports icon, title, description, action props
items.verification: Check multiple empty state scenarios; verify consistent styling

items.title: Enforce useFormatters Composable Usage
items.description: 30+ files have inline formatters (toLocaleString, toFixed, manual date formatting) instead of using the existing useFormatters composable. Standardize all formatting to use the composable.
steps: Create ESLint rule or grep pattern to identify inline formatting
Generate list of all files with inline formatters
Update each file to import and use useFormatters
Remove inline formatting functions
Add formatPhoneNumber to useFormatters if not present
Document formatters in README
definition_of_done: ESLint rule added to prevent future inline formatting
items.verification: Grep for toLocaleString and manual date formatting; should find only useFormatters

items.title: Extract Shared Controller Logic to Trait
items.description: getLandlordId() is duplicated in 9+ controllers (FinancesController, PaymentController, PaymentsHubController, OperationsHubController, WaterHubController, TenantsHubController, ArchiveHubController, MaintenanceHubController, etc.). getBuildings() is duplicated in 7+ controllers. Extract to shared trait.
steps: Create WithLandlordScope trait with getLandlordId(), getBuildings(), getProperties()
Add trait to all controllers with duplicated methods
Remove duplicated methods from individual controllers
Ensure consistent authorization checks in trait
Add unit tests for trait methods
Consider middleware alternative for auth check
definition_of_ready: All duplicated methods identified and documented
Consistent behavior confirmed across all implementations
acceptance_criteria: Single source of truth for getLandlordId()
Single source of truth for getBuildings()
All controllers use the shared trait
No code duplication for these methods
definition_of_done: Trait created with all shared methods
items.verification: grep for 'private function getLandlordId' should return only trait

items.title: Create Missing Authorization Policies
items.description: Policies are missing for Expense, LateFeePolicy, Deposit, ExpenseCategory, Vendor, WaterSetting models. Manual auth checks (33+ instances) in controllers should use policies.
steps: Create ExpensePolicy with viewAny, view, create, update, delete methods
Create LateFeeRulePolicy for late fee rules configuration
Create DepositPolicy for deposit transactions
Create ExpenseCategoryPolicy for expense categorization
Create VendorPolicy for vendor management
Create WaterSettingPolicy for water rate configuration
Register all policies in AuthServiceProvider
Migrate manual auth checks in controllers to policy calls
acceptance_criteria: Every model with CRUD operations has corresponding policy
Controllers use $this->authorize() instead of manual checks
definition_of_done: Manual auth checks migrated to policy calls
items.verification: Try accessing expense as wrong landlord; verify 403 response

items.title: Add Authorization to PaymentLinkController
items.description: PaymentLinkController::show() has minimal authorization. While payment links are public by design (signed URLs), there should be rate limiting and validation to prevent abuse and token brute-forcing.
steps: Add rate limiting middleware to payment link routes
Implement signed URL validation (already using token)
Add honeypot field for bot detection
Log suspicious access patterns
Add CSRF protection where applicable
Consider IP-based rate limiting for repeated failures
definition_of_ready: Threat model for payment links defined
acceptance_criteria: Rate limiting prevents brute force token guessing
items.verification: Attempt 100 rapid requests to payment link; verify rate limit triggers

items.title: Extract Validation Rules to FormRequest Classes
items.description: 50+ inline $request->validate() calls in controllers duplicate validation rules. Extract to FormRequest classes for reusability, testability, and cleaner controllers.
steps: Identify all inline validation in controllers (50+ instances)
Group by domain: payments, tenants, water readings, expenses, etc.
Create FormRequest classes for each domain
Migrate controller validation to FormRequest type hints
Ensure custom error messages are preserved
Add validation unit tests
acceptance_criteria: FormRequest classes for all validated endpoints
definition_of_done: No code duplication in validation rules
items.verification: Submit invalid data to endpoint; verify correct validation error returned

items.title: Centralize Payment Methods Configuration
items.description: Payment methods array is hardcoded in 3+ places (PaymentConfiguration, frontend components, validation rules). Centralize to single source of truth.
steps: Audit all payment method definitions in codebase
Create PaymentMethod enum with all valid methods
Update PaymentConfiguration to use enum
Create API endpoint for frontend to fetch payment methods
Update frontend to fetch from API instead of hardcoding
Update all validation rules to reference enum
acceptance_criteria: Single PaymentMethod enum is source of truth
Frontend fetches from API, no hardcoding
Adding new payment method requires single code change
Type safety in PHP and TypeScript
items.verification: Add new payment method to enum; verify it appears in all UIs

items.title: Split FinancesController into Domain Controllers
items.description: FinancesController is 1278 lines with mixed concerns (invoices, payments, expenses, deposits, reports, settings). Split into focused domain controllers following single responsibility principle.
steps: Analyze FinancesController methods and group by domain
Create Finance/InvoicesController for invoice operations
Create Finance/ExpensesController for expense operations
Create Finance/ReportsController for report generation
Create Finance/SettingsController for settings management
Keep FinancesController as thin orchestrator for index/hub view
Update routes to use new controllers
Preserve URL structure using route model binding
definition_of_ready: Route structure decided (preserve URLs or restructure)
acceptance_criteria: Routes continue to work (or redirect properly)
items.verification: Access all Finance pages; verify functionality unchanged

items.title: Standardize Invoice Status Constants
items.description: Invoice status strings are used inconsistently across the codebase. Create enum and use consistently throughout for type safety.
steps: Create InvoiceStatus enum with all valid statuses
Update Invoice model to use enum cast
Update all status comparisons to use enum
Update frontend to use typed status values
Add validation for status transitions
Update tests
acceptance_criteria: InvoiceStatus enum is single source of truth
All comparisons use enum, not strings
items.verification: Attempt invalid status transition; verify rejection

items.title: Remove Console Statements from Production Code
items.description: 50+ console.log/console.error statements exist in production Vue code. Remove or replace with proper logging/error handling service.
steps: Grep for all console.* statements in resources/js
Categorize: debug logs vs error handling
Remove debug logs entirely
Replace error logging with proper error service integration
Add ESLint rule to prevent future console statements
Create useErrorHandler composable for consistent error handling
acceptance_criteria: Errors reported to monitoring service or handled gracefully
ESLint rule prevents new console statements
Development-only logging preserved via env flag
definition_of_done: All console statements removed or replaced
items.verification: Build production bundle; grep for console. should return 0 matches

items.title: Make Water Rate Configurable
items.description: Water rate is hardcoded to 150 KES per unit in WaterReadingObserver. Should be configurable per landlord/building via settings.
steps: Verify PaymentConfiguration already has water_unit_rate field
Ensure WaterReadingObserver uses configured rate, not hardcoded 150
Remove all hardcoded 150 references for water rate
Add UI in Water Settings to configure rate per building
Support building-level overrides with fallback to landlord default
Update documentation
definition_of_ready: Business rules for rate inheritance defined
acceptance_criteria: No hardcoded 150 in codebase for water rate
Default rate is sensible (configurable via env)
items.verification: Change water rate in settings; verify new reading uses new rate

items.title: Create Typed Exception Classes
items.description: 21+ generic Exception throws exist without typed exceptions. Create domain-specific exception classes for better error handling and API responses.
steps: Audit all throw new Exception calls
Group by domain: Payment, Import, Invoice, Refund, etc.
Create app/Exceptions directory structure if needed
Create PaymentException, ImportException, InvoiceException, RefundException
Add error codes and context to exceptions
Update throws to use typed exceptions
Add exception handler for custom exceptions
acceptance_criteria: No generic Exception throws in services
Each domain has typed exception class
Exceptions have error codes for API clients
items.verification: Trigger payment failure; verify PaymentException with error code returned

items.title: Add TypeScript Interfaces for Vue Props
items.description: Multiple Vue components use Object type for props instead of proper TypeScript interfaces. Add proper typing for better IDE support and compile-time error catching.
steps: Audit all defineProps with Object type
Create interfaces in types/ directory for each entity
Update components to use typed props
Enable strict TypeScript checks for props
Add interface documentation
acceptance_criteria: All props have explicit interface types
definition_of_done: All components with Object props updated
items.verification: Pass wrong prop type to component; verify TypeScript error

items.title: Fix N+1 Queries in PaymentController Bulk Import
items.description: PaymentController bulk import has N+1 query pattern - units are loaded individually in loop. Use eager loading and keyed collections for O(1) lookup.
steps: Profile bulk import to identify all N+1 queries
Pre-load all units for building before loop (partially done)
Pre-load all leases with tenants
Use keyed collections for O(1) lookup
Add query count assertion to test
Consider chunk processing for large imports
definition_of_ready: Current query count for bulk import measured
acceptance_criteria: Query count is O(1), not O(n) for n payments
items.verification: Import 100 payments with query logging; verify < 20 queries

items.title: Add Caching for Finance Statistics
items.description: Finance hub stats are computed fresh on every request. Add caching with cache invalidation on relevant events to improve page load times.
steps: Identify all stats computed in FinanceStatsService
Add cache layer with appropriate TTL (5-15 minutes)
Implement cache invalidation on payment/invoice events
Add cache tags for granular invalidation
Create CacheInvalidator listener for finance events
Add cache warmup command for cold start
acceptance_criteria: Stale data window is acceptable (< 5 minutes)
Cache warming available for cold start
items.verification: Load finance hub twice; second load shows cache hit in < 50ms

items.title: Remove or Integrate Orphaned Components
items.description: QuickActionsPanel and BuildingMap components appear to be orphaned (not used anywhere). Either remove or integrate properly.
steps: Search codebase for usages of QuickActionsPanel
Search codebase for usages of BuildingMap
If unused: delete components
If planned for future: create ticket for integration
Document decision in ADR
acceptance_criteria: All components either used or deleted
definition_of_done: No dead code in components directory
items.verification: Grep for component names; verify either deleted or used

items.title: Standardize Modal Implementation
items.description: Some pages use raw HTML modals while others use the Modal component. Standardize all modals to use the shared Modal component for consistency and accessibility.
steps: Audit all modal implementations in codebase
Identify raw HTML modals not using Modal.vue
Migrate each to use Modal component
Ensure consistent styling and behavior
Add accessibility attributes (aria-modal, role)
Test keyboard navigation (Escape to close)
items.verification: Open modal; press Escape; verify close behavior

progressTracking.description: Track progress in progress.md file, committed to repo after each task

format.blockers: Any blockers or notes for next iteration

metrics: Zero duplicate MetricCard, Pagination, or Badge components
All models have corresponding authorization policies
Zero console.log in production Vue code
TypeScript build passes with no errors
Single location for notification provider configuration
Single location for notification default preferences
